title: D5 Preact+Vite SPA + production packaging
description: Create packages/dashboard/ as a new monorepo package with Preact + Vite. Implement 3 views: RulesTree (rules-tree.tsx), HumanLockVault (human-lock.tsx), IntentTimeline (intent-timeline.tsx). Add api/client.ts with fetch wrappers for all /api/* endpoints. Add hooks/use-events.ts for SSE subscription. Configure vite.config.ts proxy (/api, /mcp, /events → :7373 for dev). Production build: Vite build → dist/; configure packages/server/tsup.config.ts to copy dist/ into server/dist/static/. Implementation MUST follow the ui-design/ artifacts from TASK-005.

files.change: New package.json: name '@fabric/fabric-dashboard', private: true; devDependencies: preact, @preact/signals, vite, @preact/preset-vite; dependencies: none (api client is fetch-based); scripts: dev/build/preview

files.target: Vite config with @preact/preset-vite and dev proxy
files.change: preact() plugin; server.proxy: '/api' → 'http://127.0.0.1:7373', '/mcp' → 'http://127.0.0.1:7373', '/events' → 'http://127.0.0.1:7373' (ws: false for SSE); build.outDir: 'dist'
Import render from preact; render(<App />, document.getElementById('app'))

files.target: App root with hash-based routing for 3 views
files.change: Simple hash router (#/rules, #/locks, #/timeline); Nav component with 3 tab links; render active view based on hash; global SSE connection via use-events hook

Fetches GET /api/rules; renders tree: L0 root node (project name), L1 domain nodes, L2 file nodes; human_locked_nearby nodes get amber highlight; stale badge (red dot) if drift detected; expand/collapse per design from ui-design/rules-tree.html

Fetches GET /api/human-lock; renders card list; each card: file path, line range, locked preview snippet, drift badge (hash mismatch), Approve button (POST /api/human-lock/approve); approved state updates card to green; per design from ui-design/human-lock.html

Fetches GET /api/ledger; splits into ai/human columns; sorted by ts desc; AI column: commit_sha badge, diff_stat, rule_ref; Human column: annotation text, timestamp, Annotate button (POST /api/intent/annotate inline form); per design from ui-design/intent-timeline.html

files.target: Typed fetch wrappers for all REST endpoints
files.change: Functions: getRules(), getLedger(source?, since?), getScan(), getHumanLock(), approveHumanLock(body), annotateIntent(body), getEvents() (returns EventSource); uses @fabric/shared types if importable or inlines compatible types
useEvents(): subscribes to /events EventSource; dispatches FabricEvent to signal/state store; auto-reconnect on error; cleanup on unmount; triggers re-fetch in dependent views on relevant event type

Standard Vite HTML:
+

files.target: Copy dashboard dist/ to server/dist/static/ on build
files.change: Add onSuccess hook or esbuildPlugins that copies packages/dashboard/dist/ to dist/static/ after server build; add express.static('dist/static') route in http.ts static.ts or directly in createFabricHttpApp
app.use(express.static(path.resolve(__dirname, '../static'))); app.get('*', (req, res) => res.sendFile(path.resolve(__dirname,'../static/index.html'))) for SPA client-side routing fallback

implementation: Scaffold packages/dashboard with pnpm create vite or manual package.json + vite.config.ts; add preact + @preact/preset-vite
Implement design tokens from ui-design/tokens.json as CSS custom properties in packages/dashboard/src/styles/tokens.css
Implement 3 view components following ui-design/ HTML prototypes and component-inventory.md component specs
Implement api/client.ts typed fetch functions + hooks/use-events.ts SSE hook
Configure vite.config.ts proxy for local dev; verify pnpm dev at :5173 with fab serve at :7373
Configure production build: packages/server/tsup.config.ts or pnpm workspace build script to copy packages/dashboard/dist → packages/server/dist/static
Verify production: pnpm -r build then fab serve — browser at http://127.0.0.1:7373 shows SPA

reference.pattern: Preact + Vite SPA in monorepo package
reference.examples: Use @fabric/shared types for API response types where importable; Preact signals (@preact/signals) for reactive state; follow component-inventory.md for exact component names and props

criteria: pnpm -r build passes (all packages including dashboard build without error)
cd packages/dashboard && pnpm dev opens at :5173 and shows navigation with 3 tabs
Browser at http://127.0.0.1:7373 (production) shows complete SPA with 3 views loading data
RulesTree view renders tree hierarchy from /api/rules with expand/collapse
HumanLockVault Approve button posts to /api/human-lock/approve and updates card state
IntentTimeline shows dual-column layout with AI and Human entries from /api/ledger
SSE event from /events triggers data refresh in active view within 1s of file change
fab serve (no separate Vite dev server) serves complete SPA — single command production mode

rationale.chosen_approach: Preact + Vite as decided in analysis Round 2; independent packages/dashboard package with proxy-based dev workflow keeps server and dashboard decoupled during development
alternatives_considered: HTMX (server-side templating doesn't fit SPA audit view)
decision_factors: Preact 3KB vs React 40KB — fits maintenance-tool bundle budget
Vite HMR for fast frontend DX
Independent package allows publishing dashboard separately in future
rationale.tradeoffs: Two-terminal dev workflow (fab serve + pnpm dev) vs single-terminal prod; production bundling via tsup copy adds a build coupling between packages

manual_checks: pnpm -r build: verify packages/server/dist/static/ contains index.html and assets/
fab serve --target /path: open http://127.0.0.1:7373 and verify 3-view SPA loads
Navigate to HumanLockVault: deliberately modify a @HUMAN section, verify drift card appears
Click Approve: verify human-lock.json updated and card shows approved state
Navigate to IntentTimeline: run pre-commit, verify new AI entry appears via SSE without page reload
success_metrics: SPA loads in < 3s on first paint
All 3 views load and display real data from running fab serve
pnpm -r build succeeds for all packages

risks.description: tsup copy of dashboard dist is order-dependent (dashboard must build before server)
risks.mitigation: Add 'preinstall': 'pnpm --filter @fabric/fabric-dashboard build' to server build script; or use pnpm workspace topological order
risks.fallback: Use runtime __dirname/../dashboard-dist path lookup (server reads from sibling directory at runtime)
